cot_prefix: Okay, let's look at the question. The question is about why Babbage failed to continue his research work in 1828. The options are A through D. First, I need to read the passage again to understand the context. The passage says that Babbage was working in London for four years after exhibiting his invention in 1822. Then his health broke down and he had to take a long holiday abroad. When he returned in 1828, he was at the end of his resources. Many bills remained unpaid. His chief assistant and co-workers quarreled with him and left with many expensive tools. For one year no work was done. So the question is about the main reason why he couldn't continue his research in 1828. Let's break down the possible reasons mentioned: 1. His health broke down, leading to a long holiday abroad. That's before 1828. The problem is in 1828, so maybe health is not the main issue anymore since he returned from his holiday then. The text says "when he returned to London in 1828, he was at the end of his resources." So the key point here is "end of his resources." That would imply money issues. 2. The passage mentions that he had unpaid bills. So B is an option. However, the next part says his co-workers quarreled with him and left. But the question is about the main reason. The text states that after returning in 1828, his resources were at an end. So the main reason is he ran out of money. The other factors like co-workers leaving might be consequences or contributing factors, but the primary cause is that he was out of money. The paragraph says "he was at the end of his resources. Many bills remained unpaid. His chief assistant and co-workers quarreled with him and left..." which suggests that the financial situation led to the other issues. So the main reason is B, he almost ran out of money. The other options: A is about health, but he was back from his health break by 1828. C is about co-workers arguing, which is mentioned but it's after the fact of running out of money. D says he spent all on bills, but the text says bills remained unpaid, implying he couldn't pay them, not that
